tokens: Want me to re-align that LCRU (meaning the high-gain antenna), Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did you say my SCB's about full, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me get yours off your back right now so I can go use it to sample with.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They ought to be in there now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can't do nothing with it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Got to go down a little more.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I got them about in azimuth.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I see them; and you need to come more to your...There you go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now, you just tilted the whole works, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'll go get it from this side.
Push it over towards me, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (How come) we're having so much trouble with it this time?
Hey, you got a signal that's about...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Stops to listen) Leave it there; that's good enough.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, let's just live with that.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, it (meaning the signal strength)'s going down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (We) spend 20 minutes adjusting the TV and then turn it off (when John drives out to the final parking place).
(Pause) Going back out to the right, here, (to sample).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I want to dust it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, when you're ready, I've got a new UV-(camera) setting.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Charlie, you can continue sampling for about five more minutes, and then we'll have to load up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'll be about out of bags then, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, with the new UV setting...Wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I'll say one thing, that the character of the regolith has really changed between here and Stone Mountain and...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and your azimuth is 275, and the elevation is 66.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; there's a grab sample in 18.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, it's partially documented, I should say, not a grab sample.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, I hope this baby works.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: After this rock, we'd like you to start closing out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Tony, the last one I pick up is a(n) igneous rock, no breccia.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it's got lath crystals in it and a black matrix, but it is not basaltic.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, it was going in bag 19; it's not anymore.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll come over and help you, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: He wants us to close out now, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I guess we're through with those samples.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Chuckles as he looks at the checklist) Houston, this dust is just like an abrasive.
Any time you rub something, you can no longer read it.
And that's what's happened to our RCUs and our...(pause) every piece of gear we've got.
In other words, it's a mistake to rub something to clean it off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we're starting a little bit ahead of time on the closeout here, and we'd like you to take your time and make sure we get everything.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You can bet we're not going to leave anything.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston while he works at the LMP seat) Okay, Tony.
The core tubes are going in bag (SCB) number 7.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And it's hardly got any rocks in it.
John, you want to off-load mine?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: "EMU malfunctions," I don't want that page.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Making a mis-identification) We're with you, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, LRV config...We got...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Turning to see what John is doing) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What we gonna do with it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Just put it in there, and I'll fill up the rocks.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Want to pull my PLSS tool carrier?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm glad we didn't have an emergency; (chuckling) "quick release pin".
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's the one that we couldn't get snapped inside...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...you might try to keep a hold of the sample bags for when you go out and park the Rover.
We may have you pick up a rock and put it on the LPM.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) You want me...I'll take my camera with me, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I guess I got enough to cover it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You're not gonna drive out there yet, are you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To himself) (Tried to) put it in my teeth.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They said they were through with them.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, here's one right over here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They're down to the left of the LM, there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I('ve) got to go get that SRC.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (I'll) just leave it under your seat.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've got to get rid of this LCRU pallet so I can stick the...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If he is, tell him...(listens) If he is, tell him 'Sixty-Four Charlie' just topped the Mount Whitney event.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: He evidently knows what you're talking about.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Long Pause) (To himself) Got that ETB to left floorboard?
(Garbled) (Long Pause) The (garbled, possibly "hot rock") is already in the spacecraft.
You don't know how much fun this has been.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I think it's been obvious in your voice how much fun it's been.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Charlie, any time you put anything in the spacecraft, would you call it off?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm putting things in the ETB right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Could you call it off as you put it in there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: All the film that we brought out, except the mag on the DAC and the two mags on the camera.
Magazine F and magazine Echo are still on the cameras.
John's seat bag is empty of film.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston, the cosmic ray experiments plate will not pull out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There isn't even a strap to pull it out with.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If you hold it upright and shake it, do you think it will come out the bottom?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, why don't you try that?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Why don't you pull and let me hold the thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me hold it - the frame - and you pull on it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, but there's no string to hold on to.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You want me to get them for you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Get it so I can get the thing open.
So I can get the first frame out, then we can get it.
(Pause) I don't know, it may gonna be a struggle all the way.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I didn't see it move at all.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It moved...It looked as though it came out some.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let's hit it a couple of times.
Looks like to me the thing is so...This thing is hot, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There we go, there we go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think you got it now, babe.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) Can you stick it back in there (on the MESA)?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston, as he heads for the LMP seat) Hey, that...Thanks for those pliers, boy.
I could feel that through my
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The first panel is black at 180 degrees F.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's on the back, and it was, of course, facing right into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I got the magazine off the DAC, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Reading) "ETB; DAC magazine T to DAC." Okay.
(To Tony) You don't want me to put any magazine T on the DAC, do you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If it's out of battery (power), there's no point.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, we're not gonna do that Descartes Olympics thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: So let's not put anything on the DAC.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, the temp(erature) plate (also known as a "tempa-label") on the top one...(Stops to listen to Tony)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm just gonna leave it on the...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The "temp" plate on the top one is black at a 160 degrees F.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the A "temp" plate on the top is black at 120 and gray at 160.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: So it must have stayed relatively cool.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That thing (meaning the frame of the cosmic ray experiment) was hot, I tell you.
When I had the frame, after 5 or however many minutes there, I could start feeling it through my
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, those pliers are going to hang you up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, just throw it back in there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's going to get hung up...Climbing up.
(Reading) Okay, H; I'm gonna take the mags off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony; we're not doing any Grand Prix anymore, are we?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm gonna retrieve the cosmic ray (means "solar wind") now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, I just retrieved it (meaning the cosmic ray experiment).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I don't mean the cosmic ray, I mean the SWC.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Cosmic ray detector is bagged, and as near as I can tell, there's no thumbprints on it...on any of the plates.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This thing's got a mind of (it's own)...It's wild, Tony!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Off-camera, throwing something, possibly the cosmic ray frame) Look at that, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Won't win any world's record, but...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This thing, when it wound up, I tried to...It got away from me, and it tore just a little bit, but I think we'll be able to get it in the bag.
(It tore) up at the upper part.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Didn't wind up like a window shade.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's okay; it'll work fine that way.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; I got it wound up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, I understand there's a tempa-label on the CRE (Cosmic Ray Experiment) bag.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: In fact, I'll get it right now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Charlie; and remember (to get) the drum on the penetrometer.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I put the big rock bag on the ladder hook.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It (meaning the penetrometer drum) came off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) Did you get the big rock out of the footpad?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Picking up a rock bag near the ladder) Okay, Charlie.
Is this bag here for to go inside?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Turning to look) I didn't put it there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's one that was there from last time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'll throw that under the LM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got two full rock bags and one that's partially full, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (picking up another rock bag) We got (numbers) 4 and 6 are full, and 7 has got two core tubes and a...(Heading for the ladder with the two bags) Excuse me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, this (LM) shadow is almost gone.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Now at Charlie's Rover seat) Are those core tubes...(Do) they got anything in them?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, are you finished...Let me get one more picture of you dirty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Are you sure you want a record of that (meaning John's dirty suit)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I've got the magazine from the...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Got the DACs, all the mags...(Stops to listen) Say again.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I just said go ahead, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Going to his seat) Only thing we don't have in the ETB that I see is the CRE (Cosmic Ray Experiment), and that's going in right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What...Tony, what...(Pause as he takes the ETB to the MESA) Hey, John, you're taking a camera with a magazine out with you (to the final Rover parking spot)?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I got one more rock here that I was looking at out of the window of the lunar module.
And I got plenty of pictures of it (from the LM window) so you...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Put it in the big rock bag.
(Seeing the size of the rock) Oh, it'll go in that ETB.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, I'm gonna put magazine Foxtrot (aka Mag 117) into the ETB, and it's got 130 frames exposed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Charlie; you should have...(Pause) Charlie; you should have...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...seven Hasselblads and three DAC magazines...Actually be six in there right now; one's still on John's camera.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Where's the bag that the good old UV (having trouble finding the right word)...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, Charlie, did you throw my (Hasselblad) camera away?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No, I didn't throw your camera away.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause as John turns a page on his checklist)) The UV (film cannister) bag is upstairs (in the cabin).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (looking in the ETB to make sure they have everything) We('ve) got one, two, three, four, five, six Hasselblad (magazines); three DACs; an SWC; and a CRE; the maps; various other things.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, and that's in there, too.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Sounds like you got it all.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, John; your mags go in there, John, and that's it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me drive this (Rover) up the hill (to the VIP site about 100 meters east of the LM).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, a special salute from me to the United States Air Force on their silver (25th) anniversary this year.
(Pause) From one of the boys in blue that's pretty far out right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Looking at his wristwatch, which has been in stopwatch mode since the start of the EVA) Okay, John.
We got 4 hours and 15 minutes or so.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Looking at his checklist) Except for...I'll start taking the...As you turn...The LRV configure, you're gonna do.
The DAC; driving; SWC; astro actions, we're gonna bypass.
We got to clean the EMUs and stow antennas before you drive off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll tell you what, why don't you park the Rover while I make sure everything's under the LM, and we'll dust when we get back.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Joining John) And I'll just take a couple of SCBs and take them in...(Reaching for the dustbrush) Here, I'll bring...Oh, you got to get that, don't you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You got to get that LPM (Lunar Portable Magnetometer) out there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm gonna park the Rover (at the VIP site), Houston.
I think we got everything out of it there is to get, okay?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We were gonna do a bunch of exercises that we had made up as the Lunar Olympics to show you what a guy could do on the Moon with a backpack on, but...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: For a 380-pound guy, that's pretty good.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I jump flat-footed straight in the air, 300...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Doing his own jump and realizing he's going over backwards) Wow!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That ain't any fun, is it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Apparently trying to get onto his hands and knees) Well, I'm sorry about that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now we do have some work to do (brushing Charlie's suit).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I want to park the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well...No, you're too dirty to go up there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm just taking some bags up, okay?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Do you want the LCRU to switch 1 when we start out there, right Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Hears Tony as he goes to the LCR at the front of the Rover) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm going to be taking the SCBs up onto the porch.
transcript.speaker: Duke
